nodes.label: ∅ | stand | cabin
nodes.sentence: In the heart of an ancient Germanic forest stood a humble cabin where an old man lived with his grandson.

nodes.label: parent | lose | terror
nodes.sentence: The boy’s parents and kin had long ago been lost to the terror of six towering giants who roamed the Black Forest.

nodes.label: ∅ | destine | ∅
nodes.sentence: The tribe whispered of an old prophecy: one day, a hero would arise, marked by a single white feather, destined to astonish all with his courage and skill.

nodes.label: grandfather | give | bow
nodes.sentence: As soon as the boy was able to walk, his grandfather gave him a small bow and arrows.

nodes.label: boy | spot | creature
nodes.sentence: One day, near the forest edge, the boy spotted a curious creature—small, swift, with soft fur.

nodes.label: he | run | ∅
nodes.sentence: Unsure of what it was, he ran home to describe it.

nodes.label: grandfather | explain | ∅
nodes.sentence: His grandfather explained it was a hare, good to eat if he could only hit it with his arrow.

nodes.label: boy | try | ∅
nodes.sentence: The boy tried and succeeded, bringing it back to boil for a modest feast.

nodes.label: grandfather | teach | he
nodes.sentence: Encouraged, his grandfather taught him to hunt deer and boar, until the boy became a master woodsman.

nodes.label: curiosity | grow | life
nodes.sentence: Isolated from other villages, the boy’s curiosity grew about life beyond the trees.

nodes.label: he | find | ash
nodes.sentence: One day, at the edge of a misty clearing, he found scattered ashes and upright poles—signs of a nearby homestead unfamiliar to him.

nodes.label: man | deny | it
nodes.sentence: Asked if his grandfather had prepared them, the old man firmly denied it, warning the boy’s imagination was playing tricks.

nodes.label: voice | echo | tree
nodes.sentence: Later, as the young man ventured deeper into the forest on a solitary walk, a mysterious voice echoed through the trees: “Come forth, wearer of the white feather.

nodes.label: you | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: Though you do not yet bear it, you are worthy.”

nodes.label: voice | tell | he
nodes.sentence: The voice told him to rest and dream.

nodes.label: he | see | pipe
nodes.sentence: In this vision, he saw a pipe, a pouch for tobacco, and a large white feather.

nodes.label: gift | lie | he
nodes.sentence: Upon waking, these gifts lay before him.

nodes.label: voice | urge | ∅
nodes.sentence: “Place the feather upon your head,” the voice urged, “and you shall become a great hunter and warrior, capable of astounding feats.

nodes.label: smoke | form | flock
nodes.sentence: When you smoke the pipe, the smoke will form flocks of doves.”

nodes.label: voice | reveal | itself
nodes.sentence: The voice revealed itself to be a woodland spirit, rooted like an ancient tree, who confided that the grandfather’s kindness concealed selfish motives.

nodes.label: spirit | give | vine
nodes.sentence: The spirit gave the young man an enchanted vine, invisible to enemies, to be used in races against his foes.

nodes.label: he | triumph | ∅
nodes.sentence: If he could ensnare them with the vine during a contest of speed, he would triumph.

nodes.label: boy | glimpse | face
nodes.sentence: As the voice faded, the boy glimpsed the spirit’s face vanish like dawn mist.

nodes.label: he | return | ∅
nodes.sentence: He returned home, slept, and woke to find the pipe, pouch, and feather as promised.

nodes.label: grandfather | recall | prophecy
nodes.sentence: His grandfather, noting the white feather and the fluttering doves escaping the cabin, recalled the old prophecy with dread, fearing his hold over the boy weakening.

nodes.label: word | reach | giant
nodes.sentence: Word of his coming reached the giants, who mocked him at first, calling him a mere child with his white plume.

nodes.label: they | offer | challenge
nodes.sentence: Yet, they offered him a challenge: to race against each of them one by one.

nodes.label: course | run | tree
nodes.sentence: The course was run to a marked birch tree facing east and back to a heavy iron club that served as the prize.

nodes.label: ∅ | claim | club
nodes.sentence: Whoever won the race claimed the club and could strike down the loser.

nodes.label: feather | outpace | giant
nodes.sentence: Confident and supported by the magical vine, White Feather outpaced the first giant, ensnared him, and struck his head from his shoulders.

nodes.label: he | defeat | giant
nodes.sentence: Morning after morning, he defeated each giant in turn, claiming their heads as trophies.

nodes.label: giant | propose | trap
nodes.sentence: The last giant, cunning and wary, proposed a trap.

nodes.label: he | offer | ∅
nodes.sentence: He offered White Feather a generous head start if he would leave all the heads behind.

nodes.label: feather | refuse | ∅
nodes.sentence: White Feather refused, proud of his hard-won trophies.

nodes.label: he | meet | woman
nodes.sentence: Before confronting this final giant, White Feather met the wise forest spirit again, who warned him of a great danger: on his path he would meet a bewitching woman—the sixth giant in disguise—who would try to lure him.

nodes.label: he | shut | eye
nodes.sentence: On meeting her, he must shut his eyes and wish to become an elk to avoid her spell.

nodes.label: she | claim | ∅
nodes.sentence: She claimed to have traveled far to be his bride, admiring his bravery.

nodes.label: feather | reclaim | form
nodes.sentence: Enchanted by her beauty and sweet words, White Feather reclaimed his human form.

nodes.label: they | rest | ∅
nodes.sentence: They rested together, and as he fell into a deep sleep, the woman revealed her true nature.

nodes.label: she | transform | giant
nodes.sentence: She transformed back into the giant, seized the magic feather from his brow, struck him with the iron club, and turned him into a dog.

nodes.label: he | lead | form
nodes.sentence: In this form, he was led like a pet back to the village.

nodes.label: ∅ | live | sister
nodes.sentence: Not far away in a nearby village lived two sisters, daughters of a chieftain, known rivals who fasted for power, each hoping to win the favor of White Feather.

nodes.label: giant | visit | they
nodes.sentence: The giant, now pretending to be White Feather with the stolen feather, visited them.

nodes.label: sister | welcome | he
nodes.sentence: The elder sister, decked in finery, welcomed him lavishly and married him.

nodes.label: sister | take | dog
nodes.sentence: The younger sister, humble and kind, took in the dog, feeding and caring for him lovingly.

nodes.label: feather | hold | power
nodes.sentence: Believing the feather held all its power for whoever bore it, the giant boldly hunted on the open fields, loudly calling game to him, but frightened all animals away, returning empty-handed.

nodes.label: dog | move | riverbank
nodes.sentence: The dog, however, quietly moved to a riverbank, stepping into the water to draw out a stone that transformed into a beaver.

nodes.label: giant | try | ∅
nodes.sentence: Watching secretly, the giant tried to repeat this trick, but could only raise a useless stone by his belt.

nodes.label: dog | succeed | task
nodes.sentence: Each day, the dog succeeded at every task, while the giant failed miserably.

nodes.label: pride | grow | ∅
nodes.sentence: The youngest sister’s pride in her silent companion grew, while the elder sister’s glory dimmed as she saw her husband’s shortcomings.

nodes.label: wife | journey | father
nodes.sentence: At last, the giant’s wife journeyed to her father, boasting of her husband’s wealth of sticks and stones she called bears and beavers.

nodes.label: chieftain | send | messenger
nodes.sentence: The old chieftain, suspecting a mystery, sent messengers to fetch the youngest daughter and her dog.

nodes.label: they | find | dog
nodes.sentence: To their surprise, they found not a dog, but a handsome young man—though strangely silent.

nodes.label: voice | lose | transformation
nodes.sentence: The young man’s voice was lost in the dog’s transformation, but the youngest sister prepared a steaming lodge in the old way.

nodes.label: warmth | draw | enchantment
nodes.sentence: Enclosed with heated stones and swirling vapors, the lodge’s warmth drew the enchantment from the young man, restoring his speech but not the magical plume.

nodes.label: sister | accuse | sibling
nodes.sentence: Meanwhile, the elder sister accused her sibling of harboring an idle dog as a husband, failing to mention the false bears and beavers.

nodes.label: man | give | pipe
nodes.sentence: At their gathering, the young man—now called White Feather—was given the magic pipe.

nodes.label: smoke | emerge | ∅
nodes.sentence: When the giant tried to smoke it, only smoke emerged.

nodes.label: ∅ | place | feather
nodes.sentence: When White Feather took it, placed the white feather on his brow, and smoked, great flocks of white and blue pigeons soared from the smoke, revealing the giant a fraud.

nodes.label: chieftain | order | ∅
nodes.sentence: The chieftain ordered the giant changed into a dog and driven to the village center, where boys pelted him with clubs.

nodes.label: giant | meet | end
nodes.sentence: Thus, the six giants met their end and haunted the land no more.

nodes.label: chieftain | command | man
nodes.sentence: In honor of White Feather’s victory, the chieftain commanded the young men to fashion arrows for four days.

nodes.label: feather | request | hide
nodes.sentence: White Feather requested a buffalo hide, which he carefully shredded and scattered at night across the fields.

nodes.label: he | lead | hunter
nodes.sentence: On the fifth day, he led the hunters to the prairie where a great herd of buffalo appeared, drawn by the scattered hide pieces.

nodes.label: tribe | celebrate | triumph
nodes.sentence: The tribe celebrated White Feather’s triumph with a grand feast.

nodes.label: feather | ask | wife
nodes.sentence: Having restored peace, White Feather asked his wife to seek her father’s blessing for their journey to visit his grandfather.

nodes.label: chieftain | say | ∅
nodes.sentence: The chieftain said a wife must follow her husband wherever he goes.

nodes.label: feather | step | wife
nodes.sentence: With his white feather shining and war-club in hand, White Feather stepped into the vast forest, his faithful wife at his side, ready for the next chapter of their legend.
